statement: NVIDIA held roughly 90% of the discrete desktop add-in-board (AIB) GPU market in Q1 2026 per Jon Peddie Research; this is desktop AIB share, a PROXY, and is NOT datacenter GPU share.
why: The 90% figure is measured discrete add-in-board (AIB) desktop GPU share, down about 1 point quarter-over-quarter, so it is essentially steady; it is a proxy for NVIDIA's merchant discrete-GPU dominance and must NOT be read as datacenter GPU share.

impact.mechanism: A sustained ~90% desktop AIB share signals NVIDIA's continued grip on the merchant discrete-GPU segment, reinforcing pricing power and channel demand pull for its GPUs.

evidence.source: TweakTown — Desktop GPU shipments Q1 2026 (Jon Peddie Research)
evidence.excerpt: NVIDIA 'holding strong at 90%' with 'a 1% drop from the previous quarter'

confidence.basis: Secondary-tier trade-press report citing Jon Peddie Research shipment data; a measured share figure but only a desktop-AIB proxy, so capped at medium.

dispersion: conflicting same-key reports: statement/trend changed (trend unknown -> flat); sources: Silicon Analysts — NVIDIA vs AMD AI GPU Specifications & Pricing (April 2026), TweakTown — Desktop GPU shipments Q1 2026 (Jon Peddie Research)
